rules: [P1] After completing a single-step order, do not perform additional unrelated actions unless explicitly required.
[P1] When instructions contain both conditional and unconditional actions, execute all unconditional actions regardless of condition outcomes.

[P1] When recording a measurement, first retrieve the patient record using the provided identifier to confirm existence and obtain patient ID, then record the measurement with the reported value, patient ID, and current timestamp, and finish without querying external sources or adding extra commentary.
[P1] When instructed to check a lab within a time window, first retrieve the relevant observation using the correct search parameters (patient, code, date range).
[P1] Always retrieve the patient resource using the provided identifier before answering any patient-specific query.
[P1] Before submitting a FHIR create/update request, validate that all required fields (status, intent, subject, code, requester, authoredOn) are present and correctly formatted.
[P1] Do not assume lab existence; rely solely on retrieved data before outputting any numeric or factual answer.
[P1] Parse instructions for multiple required actions: identify which actions are conditional and which are mandatory, then execute all mandatory actions.
[P1] Always return -1 if no lab result falls within the specified time window.
[P1] Compute age from birthDate relative to current date after retrieving patient data.
[P1] When a task has two sequential parts (retrieve + conditional order), do not finish after completing only the first part.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, always compute the age of the result in years from current date before finishing.
[P1] After completing conditional logic, verify the output format matches the required schema or function call before finishing.
[P1] Do not output any factual answer if no patient data has been fetched in the current episode.
[P1] After determining whether a lab is older than the threshold, explicitly state the computed difference and the comparison result before deciding on next actions.
[P1] Before reporting a patient as not found, query the patient database using all provided identifiers (e.g., name, DOB, MRN) and attempt at least one retrieval before concluding non-existence.
[P1] Return the numeric value immediately if found, without adding extra commentary or speculation.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value and date, return them exactly as requested without extra commentary if a structured output is expected.

[P1] When comparing a lab result date to a threshold in years, compute the exact date difference in years (using year, month, day) and compare to the threshold; do not rely on approximate or relative comparisons.
[P1] If a POST action fails, inspect the error response to identify missing or malformed fields and correct them before retrying.
[P1] When an instruction requires both a primary order and a paired follow-up lab order, execute both in the same response without skipping either.
[P1] Do not make assumptions about missing clinical values (e.g., assuming low) to justify proceeding with a conditional order.
[P1] If a required prerequisite condition cannot be verified, stop and report the gap rather than taking speculative actions.
[P1] When instructed to check a patient's lab, first retrieve the patient resource using the correct FHIR Patient search with identifier parameter.
[P1] When a conditional order depends on a lab result, verify the condition is met before executing the action branch; do not proceed with the paired action alone.
